name: claude-copilot
description: Claude Copilot — a framework of specialized agents, skills, commands, and hooks that give Claude persistent memory, expert methodology, and task tracking. Agents cover architecture (ta), engineering (me), QA (qa), design (sd/design), DevOps (do), documentation (doc), and knowledge onboarding (kc).
author: Claude Copilot contributors

_notes.hook_portability: Hooks reference the same scripts in .claude/hooks/ whether installed as a plugin (${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} resolves to the plugin install dir) or as a direct clone (settings.json wires the same scripts with absolute paths). No script changes were needed — only the path-reference style differs between the two wiring mechanisms.
_notes.override_layer: This plugin ships the BASE agents only (the floor). The knowledge-repo per-agent override layer (project $KNOWLEDGE_REPO_PATH -> global ~/.claude/knowledge -> base) remains a RUNTIME prompt-assembly step performed by the cc CLI at agent-invocation time. Plugin distribution does not affect this: cc extension_get reads the override/extension/skills files and merges them on top of the base agent the plugin provides. See docs/50-features/14-plugin-install.md for the floor/ceiling model.
_notes.bundled_vs_installed: This plugin bundles only prompt-layer assets (agents, skills, commands, hooks). The cc CLI (memory, skills CLI, extension resolution) and the tc CLI (task copilot) are NOT bundled — they are separate installs (see install story below). Agents call cc/tc via Bash; if those binaries are absent, memory and task features degrade but agent markdown still loads.
_notes.cc_tc_install: cc: bash tools/cc/install.sh  OR  pipx install claude-cli. tc: pip install -e tools/tc  OR  homebrew (see SETUP.md). Run 'cc doctor' after install to verify both binaries are reachable and see which features are degraded if not.
_notes.version_source: version field above is sourced from VERSION.json .framework at build/update time. Keep in sync via 'update-project' command.
